Plaintiff Theresa Murphy Notice Of Motion For, Order To Show Cause Re Contempt For The Willful Disobedience Of Lawful Judgment Quieting Title; Statement Of Facts In Re Contempt
Off Calendar Apr-18-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Theresa Murphy, T:415.637.0183, plaintiff appearing on her own behalf;
Megan C. Kelly, Esq. of Severson & Werson, T:415.677.5679, for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF THERESA MURPHY'S Motion For, Order To Show Cause Re Contempt For The Willful Disobedience Of Lawful Judgment Quieting Title, as follows:

Plaintiff Theresa Murphy's amended motion for an order to show cause re contempt is denied. "It is well established that the affidavit by which a contempt proceeding is instituted, in order to sufficiently support an adjudication of contempt, must state facts constituting the offense, otherwise the court is without jurisdiction, and the facts essential to establish jurisdiction are (1) the making of the order, (2) knowledge of the order, (3) ability of the respondent to render compliance, and (4) willful disobedience of the order." (People v. Sup. Crt. (1965) 239 Cal.App.2d 99, 104.) Here, Ms. Murphy fails to show by a preponderance of the evidence, much less beyond a reasonable doubt, that The Bank of New York Mellon is willfully violating the judgment filed January 27, 2012. Ms. Murphy contends that the Bank has no interest in the property located at11 High Street, #201 and it is engaging in improper conduct by asserting an ownership interest over the property. The January 27, 2012 judgment determined the status of title as of December 29, 2005. (Bank's Request for Judicial Notice, Ex. 10 (Murphy's Notice of Motion for Summary Judgment).) The evidence indicates that MERS subsequently transferred the deed of trust on the property to the Bank on October 10, 2012. There is insufficient evidence to establish that this transfer was fraudulent, void, and/or unenforceable. Therefore, there is insufficient evidence to find that Bank has no interest in the property and that it has been acting in contempt of the January 27, 2012 judgment.

Counsel for the bank is to prepare an order that repeats verbatim the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.

Notice Of Motion For Clerical Correction Of Judgment Filed In Dept 302 On 1/27/12
Off Calendar Nov-20-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - THERESA LYNNE MURPHY, TEL. NO. 529-2829, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. JOSEPH J. POPPEN OF BRYAN CAVE LLP, TEL. NO. 675-3470 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
ATTY. IAN ROSS OF THE LAW OFFICES OF KEESAL YOUNG & LOGAN, TEL. NO. 398-6000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS JP MORGAN CHASE BANK, MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC REGISTRATION AND U.S. BANK.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF THERESA MURPHY'S Motion For Clerical Correction Of Judgment is Denied. A final judgment may only be altered where the Court has made a "clerical mistake." (CCP sec. 473(d).) "The test which distinguishes clerical error from possible judicial error is simply whether the challenged portion of the judgment was entered inadvertently (which is clerical error) versus advertently (which might be judicial error, but is not clerical error). Unless the challenged portion of the judgment was entered inadvertently, it cannot be changed post judgment under the guise of correction of clerical error." (Bell v. Farmers Ins. Exch. (2006) 135 Cal.App.4th 1138, 1144.) Plaintiff has not established that the Court inadvertently failed to include the language she seeks to add in the amended judgment. The only cause of action at issue in the motion for summary judgment was to quiet title. The motion for summary judgment did not address the two deeds of trust at issue. (See Defendant JP Morgan Chase Bank, N.A.'s Request for Judicial Notice, Ex. 13.) A demurrer had previously been sustained without leave to amend to the cancellation of written instrument cause of action. As such, the Court can infer that the final judgment entered in this case purposefully did not address the deeds of trust and that no clerical error was committed. All requests for judicial notice are granted. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg
